mnemonics.title: B-W-B Timing Pattern
mnemonics.content: Barrier-Wtime-Barrier: The two-barrier approach. First Barrier syncs all processes, Wtime records start, work happens, second Barrier waits for all, Wtime records end.

mnemonics.title: WALL vs CPU
mnemonics.content: WALL-clock time = Waiting + Active + Latency + Load-imbalance. CPU time only counts Active. For parallel programs, always use WALL.

mnemonics.title: MPI_Wtime Returns Seconds
mnemonics.content: W for Wall-clock, returns double-precision seconds since arbitrary past point. Difference of two calls = elapsed time.

mnemonics.title: TIMER Best Practices
mnemonics.content: Trials (run multiple), Isolate (separate init from work), Minimize (timer overhead), Evaluate (use MPI_Reduce for max time), Rank-0 (one process reports).

mnemonics.title: Why Two Barriers Matter
mnemonics.content: Without Barrier-1: fast processes start timing early (inaccurate start). Without Barrier-2: master stops timing before workers finish (incomplete measurement).
